request.question: The Fiery Furnaces and Paul Westerberg, are of which nationality?

title: The Vox Jaguars
Winter Women and Holy Ghost Language School
Bitter Tea
I'm Going Away (disambiguation)
Take Me Round Again
Last Summer (album)
I'm Going Away
The Fiery Furnaces
Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg
Paul Westerberg
sentences: The Vox Jaguars was a North American rock band that formed in Santa Cruz, California in 2006.
They began their career with performances in local coffee houses and record stores.
They are influenced by artists such as The Stooges, MC5, The Velvet Underground, and other Protopunk artists.
The Vox Jaguars are well known for their energetic live performances, and have performed with The Fiery Furnaces, Big Brother & The Holding Company, Grand Ole Party, and Scissors For Lefty.
The Vox Jaguars have been part of the garage rock revival, and have influenced many indie artists in the Santa Cruz area of California (No Age, Foreign Born).
No Age said they were their "favorite new band".
Winter Women and Holy Ghost Language School are two albums by Matthew Friedberger of The Fiery Furnaces recorded, unlike The Fiery Furnaces records, without his sister Eleanor Friedberger.
Though two separate albums, they were released together as a double album by 859 Recordings in August 2006.
It was rereleased in October 2009 on Thrill Jockey Records.
Bitter Tea is the fifth full-length album by The Fiery Furnaces, released on April 18, 2006 via Fat Possum in the U.S and Rough Trade in the UK.
After it leaked onto the internet on February 22, the band immediately started selling the CD on tour.
I'm Going Away is an album by The Fiery Furnaces
Take Me Round Again is The Fiery Furnaces' ninth album.
It was first released, in MP3 format, through Thrill Jockey Records, in November 2009.
The album is being described as "The Friedbergers cover the Friedbergers,"

Last Summer is the first solo album released by American indie pop musician Eleanor Friedberger, most notable for being the vocalist in the band The Fiery Furnaces with her brother Matthew Friedberger.
The album was released on 12 July 2011 on the Merge Records record label.
I'm Going Away is The Fiery Furnaces' eighth album.
It was released, on CD and LP, on July 21, 2009 in the US and August 24, 2009 in the UK.
The Fiery Furnaces are an American indie rock band, formed in 2000 in Brooklyn, New York.
The band's primary members are Matthew and Eleanor Friedberger.
The siblings are originally from Oak Park, Illinois, a near-western suburb of Chicago.
They are known for their conceptual, highly ambitious releases, which have frequently divided critical opinion.
Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg is a compilation album of recordings by Paul Westerberg.

Paul Harold Westerberg (born December 31, 1959) is an American musician, best known as the lead singer, guitarist and songwriter in The Replacements, one of the seminal alternative rock bands of the 1980s.
He launched a solo career after the dissolution of that band.
In recent years, he has cultivated a more independent-minded approach, primarily recording his music at home in his basement.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: The Fiery Furnaces and Paul Westerberg, are of which nationality?
hop1_docs: Fiery Furnace (Arches National Park) | The Fiery Furnace is a collection of narrow sandstone canyons, fins and natural arches in Arches National Park in Utah, USA.
The Fiery Furnaces discography | Below is a listing of the official release to date by the band The Fiery Furnaces.

The Fiery Furnaces | The Fiery Furnaces are an American indie rock band, formed in 2000 in Brooklyn, New York.  The band's primary members are Matthew and Eleanor Friedberger.  The siblings are originally from Oak Park, Illinois, a near-western suburb of Chicago.  They are known for their conceptual, highly ambitious releases, which have frequently divided critical opinion.
Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg | Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg is a compilation album of recordings by Paul Westerberg.

EP (The Fiery Furnaces album) | EP is the third release from the U.S. indie rock band The Fiery Furnaces.  It is 41 minutes in length, and is arguably not an EP, but rather a short LP, and thus a proper album.

Out of the Fiery Furnace | Out of the Fiery Furnace is a seven-part documentary series which was completed for ABC television in Australia in 1985.  The series traces the discovery and use of metals, minerals, and energy resources through time and was shown in over twenty countries.  It was produced by Robert Raymond, was written by Robert Raymond and Michael Charlton and was directed by Chris McCullough.  Supported by the Rio Tinto group (via CRA Services Ltd) it consisted of seven episodes, each an hour in duration.  Australian nuclear power advocate Ian Hore-Lacy was closely involved with the production.

The Burning Fiery Furnace | The Burning Fiery Furnace is an English music drama with music composed by Benjamin Britten, his Opus 77, to a libretto by William Plomer.  One of Britten's three "Parables for Church Performances", this work received its premiere at the St Bartholomew's Church, Orford, Suffolk, England, on 9 June 1966 by the English Opera Group.

response.summary_1:
Primary topics: The Fiery Furnaces; Paul Westerberg

- The Fiery Furnaces — facts: "'an American indie rock band'"; "formed in 2000 in Brooklyn, New York"; "primary members are Matthew and Eleanor Friedberger"; "originally from Oak Park, Illinois."
- Paul Westerberg — passage evidence: "'Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg is a compilation album of recordings by Paul Westerberg'." 
- Missing / ambiguity: Paul Westerberg's nationality is not stated in the supplied passages (bridge entity: Paul Westerberg (musician)).
- Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
  1) "Paul Westerberg" — Wikipedia page lede/infobox (opening sentence; 'Born'/'Nationality'/'Origin' fields).
  2) "Paul Westerberg biography" — sections "Early life"/"Background"/infobox on biography pages (confirm nationality).
  3) "The Fiery Furnaces" — Wikipedia page lede/infobox ("Origin"/opening sentence) to confirm the band's nationality if needed.
hop2_docs: Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg | Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg is a compilation album of recordings by Paul Westerberg.

Stereo (Paul Westerberg album) | Stereo is the fourth solo album by Paul Westerberg.  At this point in his career, the former Replacements leader was entering a new phase.  He said goodbye to professional studios and big-name producers like Brendan O'Brien and Don Was, recording the album in his basement by himself.  As detailed in the liner notes, Westerberg made no effort to fix imperfections like the tape running out in the middle of a song.  "Stereo" was packaged with "Mono", which he credited to his Grandpaboy alter ego.

14 Songs (Paul Westerberg album) | 14 Songs is the first official solo album from Paul Westerberg, former leader of The Replacements.  In many ways, it's his second solo album, given that the final Replacements album, "All Shook Down", was heavily packed with session musicians and marginalized the other three band members.

Paul Westerberg | Paul Harold Westerberg (born December 31, 1959) is an American musician, best known as the lead singer, guitarist and songwriter in The Replacements, one of the seminal alternative rock bands of the 1980s.  He launched a solo career after the dissolution of that band.  In recent years, he has cultivated a more independent-minded approach, primarily recording his music at home in his basement.
Eventually (album) | Eventually is the second solo album that Paul Westerberg released after the breakup of The Replacements.
Suicaine Gratifaction | Suicaine Gratifaction is the third solo album from former The Replacements leader Paul Westerberg.

Swingin Party | "Swingin Party" is a song written by Paul Westerberg and recorded by his band The Replacements for their fourth studio album "Tim" (1985).  The song is an indie rock and rock and roll ballad with influences from jazz, country and new wave.  Lyrically, it portrays the protagonist's "feigned nonchalance".  It was well received by music critics, who praised Westerberg's songwriting talent.  The song has been covered by other artists, notably Kindness in 2009 and Lorde in 2013.
hop2_titles: Besterberg: The Best of Paul Westerberg
